title: eryngium leavenworthii
intro: Eryngium leavenworthii, also known as Leavenworth's eryngo, is a flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. It is a perennial herb that grows in the western United States, particularly in the dry regions of the Great Basin and the Mojave Desert. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, derived from its roots and leaves. The herbal preparations of Eryngium leavenworthii can be used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, and it is often consumed as a tea or used topically as a salve.
